message:
test(readme): sequential op result assertions (cumsum/prod/min/max/diff/shift/pct_change)

Existing tests called the sequential ops on DataFrame/Series but only
shape was asserted (e.g., csum.index().len()==4). Algebraic results
were uncovered — a regression in any cumulative/lag op would not
have surfaced.

Adds readme_sequential_ops_round_trip — reachable via prelude alone —
on a known input [1, 2, 3, 4]:

- cumsum  → [1, 3, 6, 10]
- cumprod → [1, 2, 6, 24]
- cummin  → [1, 1, 1, 1] (monotonic input means min stays at first)
- cummax  → [1, 2, 3, 4] (each value sets new max)
- diff(1) → [Null, 1, 1, 1] (constant first-differences)
- shift(1) → [Null, 1, 2, 3] (lag by 1 row)
- pct_change(1) row 1 → 1.0 (=(2-1)/1)

API discovery (corrected): cumsum/cumprod/diff/shift on Int64 input
may promote to Float64 in the result column. Test extracts numeric
values via a helper that accepts both Int64 and Float64 variants.

Closes frankenpandas-fd90.61.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
